mnemonics: CAPTAIN Data: Consistency, Atomicity, Persistent, Time-variant, Integrated, Non-volatile, Subject-oriented
keyPoints: Subject-Oriented: Organized around major subjects (e.g., customers, products), not applications.
Integrated: Data is unified from different sources into a consistent format.
Time-Variant: Data is stored with a time dimension to track changes over time.
Non-Volatile: Data is stable; it is loaded and accessed for analysis, not updated or deleted in real-time.
commonMistakes: Confusing a Data Warehouse with a transactional database (OLTP vs. OLAP).
Treating the Data Warehouse as a direct copy of operational data without integration and transformation.
